respawn.title: Re-summon and defeat the Ender Dragon — use 4 End Crystals to respawn it and farm Dragon Breath

subtitle.text: The Egg is the trophy. The re-summon is the farm. Four End Crystals. Four pillars. Infinite Dragon Breath.

description: CH.13 · THE DRAGON EGG
▸ The Dragon Egg drops once from the first kill.
Only once. Collect it before it falls into void.
After that: re-summon the Dragon for farming.
Dragon Breath is needed for Lingering Potions.
End's Delight (Ch14) uses Dragon Breath for cooking.
Farm more than one batch.
◈ DRAGON EGG COLLECTION
└ Dragon Egg teleports when clicked — use piston
to push it off its pedestal, collect when it falls
└ Alternative: TNT nearby (teleports on explosion proximity)
→ place TNT, ignite, collect at landing
└ One egg total across all Dragon kills
Re-summoned Dragon does NOT drop another egg
◈ DRAGON RE-SUMMON
└ Place 4 End Crystals on the edge of the bedrock portal
(one on each cardinal face of the exit portal)
└ Dragon respawns: End Crystals regenerate on pillars,
then Dragon emerges and begins attack phase
└ Re-summoned Dragon: same HP, same fight, no egg
└ Drops: Dragon Breath (collect with glass bottles
during the dragon's breath attack), XP
◈ DRAGON BREATH COLLECTION
└ Use glass bottle on the breath cloud: fills with breath
└ Collect during: death beam, ground breath attack
└ Stack: 64 dragon_breath minimum per run
└ Uses: Lingering Potion (splash + dragon_breath = lingering)
End's Delight (Ch14): required cooking ingredient
▸ The Egg does not hatch.
No civilization figured out how to hatch it.
Or they knew, and chose not to.
The Dragon Egg Research mechanic (Ch14) explores why.
This quest is just about collecting it.
That part is simpler.
▶ Collect the Dragon Egg
▶ Re-summon and defeat the Dragon again [check]
◇ End's Delight Dragon Egg Research mechanic:
uses the egg as a research trigger for all dragon recipes.
Do not cook the egg. Use it as a research item.
Full mechanic explained in Ch14.
